title: WhatsApp Platform Architecture Document

overview.purpose: Scalable WhatsApp messaging platform with clear separation of concerns

whatsapp_engine.name: WhatsApp Engine
key_features: QR code generation for device pairing

message_reception.flow: WhatsApp User → Engine → Backend → Database → Frontend
message_reception.description: Incoming messages flow through webhook processing

reply_sending.flow: Frontend → Backend → Validation → Engine → WhatsApp User
reply_sending.description: Reply messages with phone number validation and normalization

webhook_fix.issue: Invalid phone numbers stored from webhook
webhook_fix.solution: Extract phone from remote_jid instead of phone_number field

phone_validation.issue: Messages sent to invalid WhatsApp JIDs
phone_validation.solution: Strict MSISDN validation (10-15 digits) before JID construction

jid_construction.issue: JID created with raw phone instead of normalized phone

database_cleanup.solution: Cleaned all invalid records (length > 15 or non-digit)

architecture_benefits: Scalability: Each service can scale independently
Maintainability: Clear code boundaries and responsibilities
Technology Flexibility: Best tools for each service's purpose
Development Efficiency: Teams can work on services independently
Testing: Comprehensive integration and unit tests
Monitoring: Health checks and structured logging

security.session_security: Encrypted session storage, secure QR codes

optimization_points: Frontend: Code splitting, lazy loading, static asset CDN
Backend: Database indexing, connection pooling, Redis cache
